description:
You are working as an observability engineer.  
  Two raw access-log files have already been copied to your machine:

    • /home/user/logs/log_serverA.log  
    • /home/user/logs/log_serverB.log  

  Each line in both files strictly follows this 5-field, single-space-separated format  
  (there are no extra spaces and the order never changes):

    ISO-8601-timestamp hostname endpoint HTTP-status-code latency-in-milliseconds

  Example:  
    2023-10-23T08:00:01Z serverA /api/items 500 34

  Your job is to create a small, script-friendly report folder named  
    /home/user/observability  
  and produce three text files inside it.  
  Everything must be done with standard shell tools, primarily awk and sed.  
  The whole task should be solvable in roughly 5–10 reasonably short commands.

  1. /home/user/observability/errors.log  
     • Contains every single line whose HTTP status is in the 5xx range  
       (500–599, inclusive).  
     • Preserve the original order in which the lines appear in their respective
       source files: read the whole of log_serverA.log first, then the whole of
       log_serverB.log.  
     • No header line, no blank lines.

  2. /home/user/observability/error_summary.txt  
     • Two columns, single-space-separated, **no header line**:
         endpoint total_5xx_count  
     • Compute the total amount of 5xx responses per endpoint across both logs.  
     • Sort primarily by the count in descending numeric order; if two endpoints
       have the same count, sort them lexicographically (ascending).  
     • The automated test will check both the order and the exact numbers.

  3. /home/user/observability/latency_summary.txt  
     • The very first line must be the header (exactly):  
         HOSTNAME AVG_LATENCY_MS  
     • Subsequent lines contain the hostname and the average latency _only for
       HTTP 200 lines_.  
     • Average must be computed with full floating-point precision but written
       rounded to exactly two decimal places (printf "%.2f").  
     • Sort the body of the file alphabetically by hostname (ascending).

  Do not hard-code anything besides what is documented here—derive everything from
  the log files that already exist on disk.  When you are done, the three files
  must exist at the exact paths given above with precisely the contents and
  formats described, otherwise automated grading will fail.
truth:

  <file path="/home/user/logs/log_serverA.log"><![CDATA[

2023-10-23T08:00:07Z serverA /api/auth 200 110
2023-10-23T08:00:08Z serverA /api/items 200 60
2023-10-23T08:00:09Z serverA /api/items 504 40
]]></file>

  <file path="/home/user/logs/log_serverB.log"><![CDATA[
2023-10-23T08:00:00Z serverB /api/auth 200 100

]]></file>

  <!-- Expected artefacts after the agent has finished -->

  <file path="/home/user/observability/errors.log"><![CDATA[
2023-10-23T08:00:01Z serverA /api/items 500 34
2023-10-23T08:00:03Z serverA /api/cart 502 89
2023-10-23T08:00:06Z serverA /api/auth 503 45

2023-10-23T08:00:02Z serverB /api/items 500 30
2023-10-23T08:00:04Z serverB /api/cart 504 90
2023-10-23T08:00:06Z serverB /api/auth 502 55
]]></file>

/api/items 3
/api/auth 2
/api/cart 2
]]></file>

serverA 88.50
serverB 80.43
]]></file>
